description_long: This sculpture is a detailed relief depicting three human figures, with the central figure elevated and flanked by two others in postures of reverence or mourning. The central figure appears to be affixed to a cross, with draped garments and a visible ribcage, suggesting a scene of significant emotional or historical weight. The two flanking figures are dressed in long robes, with one figure bowing their head and the other gazing upward, both showing intricate folds in their clothing. The relief is carved from a single piece of pale stone, with broken and irregular edges indicating it may be a fragment of a larger work. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with fine details in the facial expressions and drapery. The back of the sculpture is flat and unadorned, emphasizing its intended display against a wall or similar surface.
description: A pale stone relief sculpture depicting three robed human figures, with the central figure elevated on a cross and flanked by two mourners. The piece is intricately carved with detailed drapery and expressive faces, and has broken, irregular edges.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full relief with all three figures clearly visible, highlighting the central crucified figure and the two mourners on either side.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the depth of the relief and the flat, unadorned back, emphasizing the three-dimensional carving of the figures.
description_view_2: A slightly angled view from above, showing the detailed drapery and the spatial arrangement of the three figures, with the central figure's posture and the mourners' gestures more apparent.
description_view_3: The opposite side view, again showing the relief's depth and the flat back, with the figures' profiles and the broken edge of the sculpture visible.
attribute_subject_matter: crucifixion scene with three human figures
attribute_location_context: No visible context; the background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
